page_title: Transit visa: Who can apply - Canada.ca

h2: Some people aren’t allowed to enter Canada
h3: Travelling to or from the United States
Who can’t apply for a transit visa

sections.heading: Eligibility
items: You need a transit visa if you’re from a visa-required country and:
bullets: your international flight stops at a Canadian airport on its way to another country
you’ll be connecting between 2 international flights at one or more Canadian airports
your transit through Canada will last 48 hours or less
you don’t have a valid visitor visa
you’re not eligible for an eTA .

sections.heading: Travelling to or from the United States
items: You may be able to transit without a visa if you hold a passport from one of the countries below and you meet certain conditions:
Find out if you can transit without a visa .
bullets: Taiwan (holder of passport without an personal identification number)

sections.heading: Who can’t apply for a transit visa
Carry valid travel documents
items: When travelling to Canada, you must have a valid passport for yourself and any children travelling with you. Passports are the only reliable and universally accepted identification document for travelling.
If you don’t have the proper documents with you, you may be:

sections.heading: Some people aren’t allowed to enter Canada
items: Some people are not admissible to Canada, which means they’re not allowed to enter the country. You can be inadmissible for several reasons, including being involved in:
You can also be inadmissible for other security, health or financial reasons.
